perturbation.original_text: AJR shall use its best efforts to promote the name, image, brand and reputation of XC and the products and services of XC. In addition, AJR shall assist XC in evaluating various applications of wearable and wireless devices to automobile racing including -2- potential applications for the use of devices at the track, in the pits, in the stands and generally for crowd control.
perturbation.changed_text: AJR is encouraged to use its best efforts to promote the name, image, brand and reputation of XC and the products and services of XC, however AJR's actions in promoting the name image brand and reputation shall not exceed $5000 without written consent of XC. In addition, AJR shall assist XC in evaluating various applications of wearable and wireless devices to automobile racing including -2- potential applications for the use of devices at the track, in the pits, in the stands and generally for crowd control.
perturbation.explanation: The change adds a financial constraint limiting AJR's promotional activities to $5000 without XC's approval. This contradicts the 'best efforts' clause, as 'best efforts' typically implies an unconstrained approach. This financial cap creates an in-text contradiction with the original intent of AJR using their 'best efforts'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AJR is encouraged to use its best efforts to promote the name, image, brand and reputation of XC and the products and services of XC, however AJR's actions in promoting the name image brand and reputation shall not exceed $5000 without written consent of XC. In addition, AJR shall assist XC in evaluating various applications of wearable and wireless devices to automobile racing including -2- potential applications for the use of devices at the track, in the pits, in the stands and generally for crowd control.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that 'AJR shall use its best efforts'. The changed text introduces a financial limitation of $5000 without written consent. 'Best efforts' implies an unconstrained effort, whereas the financial limitation directly restricts the scope of those efforts. This creates a contradiction because AJR cannot simultaneously exert 'best efforts' while being financially capped. This satisfies the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' because the term 'best efforts' is essentially being redefined by the financial limit.

perturbation.original_text: XC shall have approval rights over use of its logo by AJR and shall designate which XC trademark identifications are utilized by AJR and how they are used in connection with the logos.
perturbation.changed_text: XC shall have initial approval rights over use of its logo by AJR for the first instance. After that, AJR has full rights and control over trademark identifications, usage, and changes by AJR without any further approvals needed from XC.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text states that after the initial approval, AJR has full rights and control over trademark identifications without further approval from XC. This change introduces an internal contradiction because Section 6(a) says 'AJR shall only be permitted to use the Licensed Materials in connection with the Sponsorship and only in the limited manner expressly permitted as set forth on Schedule A attached hereto. No other use of any of the Licensed Materials by AJR shall be permitted. AJR shall not have the right to modify, alter or change any of the Licensed Materials.' This creates ambiguity about who ultimately controls the use and modification of the logos, especially given Schedule A’s restrictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XC does hereby grant AJR a limited and non-transferable license and non-exclusive right to use XC's logo and trademarks and service marks set forth on Schedule A attached hereto (the "Licensed Materials") during the Term of this Agreement. AJR shall only be permitted to use the Licensed Materials in connection with the Sponsorship and only in the limited manner expressly permitted as set forth on Schedule A attached hereto. No other use of any of the Licensed Materials by AJR shall be permitted. AJR shall not have the right to modify, alter or change any of the Licensed Materials. All Licensed Materials shall remain the sole and exclusive property of XC and AJR shall not obtain any right, title or interest therein. AJR shall not have any right to sub-license any of the Licensed Materials to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text gives AJR "full rights and control over trademark identifications, usage, and changes... without any further approvals needed from XC" after the initial approval. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that "AJR shall not have the right to modify, alter or change any of the Licensed Materials" and that "All Licensed Materials shall remain the sole and exclusive property of XC." This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding who has the ultimate authority over the use and modification of the licensed logos. The original text gave approval rights to XC. The change takes those rights away, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: AJR shall not take any action or suffer any action to occur, whether taken by the Team or others, which could result in an adverse impact on XC, its Licensed Materials and the goodwill associated therewith as a result of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: AJR shall not take any direct or intentional action that will result in an adverse impact on XC, its Licensed Materials and the goodwill associated therewith as a result of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text specifies that AJR is only responsible for 'direct or intentional' actions that cause an adverse impact on XC. This introduces an internal contradiction because in Section 6(d) states that "AJR shall not use any of the Licensed Materials to express or imply any endorsement of any other sponsor of the Team by XC. AJR shall not use any of the Licensed Materials in connection with, or in any way associated with, the names, marks, trademarks, servicemarks, symbols, products, services, logos or proprietary designations or properties of any third party." which has a broader standard and is not limited to direct or intentional actions. This means there is ambiguity about whether AJR is responsible for all actions that harm XC's reputation (original text), or only those that are direct and intentional (modified text).
perturbation.contradicted_text: AJR shall not use any of the Licensed Materials to express or imply any endorsement of any other sponsor of the Team by XC. AJR shall not use any of the Licensed Materials in connection with, or in any way associated with, the names, marks, trademarks, servicemarks, symbols, products, services, logos or proprietary designations or properties of any third party.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a 'direct or intentional' standard, while the contradicted text imposes a broader obligation without such limitations. This means AJR is now only liable for actions directly intended to harm XC, whereas the original text, in tandem with Section 6(d) (the contradicted text), held AJR accountable for a wider range of actions, including those that were not necessarily intentional, but still resulted in an adverse impact regarding endorsements or associations with third parties. This creates a strong contradiction, as AJR can now use Licensed Materials in a way that harms XC's relationship to the team's sponsors so long as it isn't 'direct or intentional'. Thus, the modified text weakens the original obligation and directly conflicts with Section 6(d).
